summary:
Gross gingival swelling is not a hallmark or common finding in pediatric acute leukemia; instead, typical signs like pallor due to anemia and purpura from thrombocytopenia are far more consistent and clinically significant. The presence of gingival swelling would be atypical and misleading if presented as a defining feature of this condition.  

Note: This summary focuses only on direct clinical observations related to presentation patterns in pediatric acute leukemia without referencing non-clinical details.
